description: Harden the autopilot §6 eval-gate to key on the green→red regression delta + runner exit code (not the bare presence of a REGRESSION row), and add an eval-gate probe that pins the corrected rule.

userStories.title: Rewrite §6 eval-gate decision rule
userStories.description: As the autopilot loop, I want my eval gate to proceed on a pre-existing red probe so that an unrelated host-state regression does not permanently block every PR from reaching ready.
acceptanceCriteria: In .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md §6, the gate PROCEEDS to §7 when BOTH: (a) the /eval runner exits 0, AND (b) every regressed probe's delta is `unchanged` vs base (already-red, not a NEW green→red transition)
The two PROCEED conditions appear as an explicit bulleted/numbered decision list, not buried in prose
The gate keeps the PR draft (PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED) only on a NEW (green→red) regression OR a non-zero runner exit
§6 includes a non-gating diagnostic note: a self-referential probe (e.g. eval-gate on an autopilot edit) with file overlap still proceeds because runner-exit + delta govern
§6 instructs posting any pre-existing red as a PR comment for honesty (no all-green claim)
The phrase "Any `REGRESSION`" no longer appears as the gate condition in §6
grep -n 'Any `REGRESSION`' .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md returns no match inside §6
userStories.notes: iter1 2026-06-11 93620ce: §6 rewritten to key on green→red delta + runner exit code; bare "Any `REGRESSION`" gate removed.

userStories.title: Align PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED status-token description
userStories.description: As a reviewer reading the autopilot reference table, I want the PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED token to match the new gate semantics so the doc is internally consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: The PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED row in the §Reference status-token table reads that the PR was left draft because /eval reported a NEW (green→red) probe regression or a non-zero runner exit
The status-token table retains its existing row count — no other rows added, removed, or reworded (verify with a `grep -c` row-count before/after)
Only the PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED row content changed
userStories.notes: iter1 2026-06-11: PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED status-token row reworded to NEW (green→red) regression or non-zero runner exit; only that row changed, table row count unchanged.

userStories.title: Add the eval-gate probe
userStories.description: As the eval suite, I want a probe that asserts the autopilot §6 rule is the corrected one so the lesson cannot silently regress.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/eval-gate.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)

It follows the existing probe pattern (evals/probes/devtcp-hook.sh): `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, header comments `# tier: A` / `# source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-11 (eval-gate)` / `# desc:`, and root resolution ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
It is a 3-state oracle: exit 0 = PASS, exit 1 = REGRESSION, exit 2 = SKIPPED; it emits a PASS/REGRESSION/SKIPPED line on stderr
It SKIPs (exit 2) if .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md is absent

PASS uses POSITIVE AND-logic assertions: the §6 region must contain the corrected-vocabulary tokens (the literal `delta` and `unchanged` and a NEW-green→red phrasing) AND must NOT contain the bare "Any `REGRESSION`" gate phrasing; REGRESSION (exit 1) if either assertion fails
bash evals/probes/eval-gate.sh exits 0 with PASS against the corrected skill (run directly)
The reverted-text check (old §6 text → exit 1) is verified inside a throwaway git worktree, never by editing the live main-checkout skill file
bash -n evals/probes/eval-gate.sh is clean (no shell syntax errors)

userStories.title: Refresh the eval benchmark
userStories.description: As the autopilot run, I want the eval benchmark refreshed so the new probe is recorded green with no new regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: /eval runs; evals/RESULTS.md contains an eval-gate row with status PASS
The /eval runner exits 0; no previously-PASS probe changes to REGRESSION/TIMEOUT/ERROR (PASS→SKIPPED for an absent-environment dependency is permitted)
eval-gate's green status is additionally confirmed by a direct bash evals/probes/eval-gate.sh → exit 0 (the delta is vacuous for a brand-new probe)
The refreshed evals/RESULTS.md is committed on the work branch via the single /eval invocation — no duplicate benchmark commit
